summary: This is an advice column post where 'Aunt Pythia' (Cathy O'Neil) answers reader questions about dating, relationships, career transitions, and exam anxiety. The most substantive exchange involves a young man seeking relationship advice, where O'Neil gently but firmly challenges his self-centered framing of his new relationship. She emphasizes that successful relationships require gratitude for one's partner and genuine pleasure in making them happy. Other questions cover compatibility assessments, leaving a PhD program for data science, and managing exam stress.
key_points: Successful relationships require two key elements: enjoying making the other person happy, and feeling grateful that they put up with you
Being too self-absorbed prevents you from developing the emotional foundation needed for lasting relationships
When evaluating career transitions (like PhD to data science), timing matters - acquiring skills before job hunting is strategic
Dating questionnaires reveal interest through participation itself, not just answers - someone filling out a 'ridiculous questionnaire' signals they're interested
Study groups are effective for exam preparation because explaining concepts to others solidifies learning and reveals how much you already know
money_quotes: I spend about half of my time being grateful that my partner puts up with me, which is probably not enough, and that gratitude makes my marriage work better.

If you are too self-absorbed, you will miss the two most crucial elements of successful relationships: first, enjoying making the other person happy and, what is the flip side of the same coin, feeling grateful that the other person will put up with you.

Falling in love is a rush, with dopamine and all that good stuff, so I'm going to guess you aren't actually falling in love. Maybe your positive feelings are just relief that you're no longer alone? That's not the same thing.
It seems like you're prematurely categorizing her as someone you won't take seriously, which I think is strange and self-defeating.
She agreed to fill out this ridiculous questionnaire, which she'd only do if she was interested, and which you'd only ask her to do if you were interested, the vital ingredient.
key_insight: The foundation of successful relationships is not passion or compatibility metrics, but cultivating genuine gratitude for your partner and finding joy in their happiness.
